title: The Red Sox have a clear formula for success. Is it sustainable?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox have their lowest batting average since 1968 and best ERA since 1992 in the current season.
The Red Sox are 14-6 when scoring four or more runs but only 8-21 with three or fewer.
Sustaining a successful season depends on improving offensive consistency while maintaining strong pitching and defense.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter found dangerous, ordered held without bail
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Tyler Brown, accused of shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, was found dangerous and ordered held without bail.
Brown faces multiple charges including assault to murder, and was recovering virtually from gunshot wounds during his court hearing.
Prosecutors highlighted Brown's extensive criminal history; his attorneys plan a robust defense at trial.

title: Isaiah Thomas hopes ‘second act’ with Celtics will help him become a GM
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Isaiah Thomas has joined the Boston Celtics as a pro and college scout based in Seattle.
Thomas aims to transition into a team executive role and potentially become a GM.
After leaving the Celtics in 2018, Thomas played with eight teams before ending his NBA career in 2024.

title: Woman pleads not guilty in teen’s 2025 drowning in N.H.’s Lake Winnipesaukee
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Buffy Ann Doyle, 50, pleaded not guilty to charges related to a 17-year-old's drowning in Lake Winnipesaukee.
She is charged with felony negligent homicide and accused of providing alcohol to the teen before his drowning.
Doyle is also accused of tampering with evidence by discarding alcohol during the police investigation.

title: Cumberland Farms makes a splash with ‘$99’ coffee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cumberland Farms mistakenly advertised coffee at $99, leading to widespread online discussion and humor about inflation.
The ad, featuring odd graphic errors, sparked speculation that it was created by AI, causing further debate.
Cumberland Farms, based in Westborough, MA, has over 1,500 stores but did not clarify the ad intention.

title: Boston City Council votes to keep recent pay raises
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council voted to maintain recent salary raises, rejecting a resolution to rescind them.
The salary increases, approved in 2022, raised councilor pay from $103,500 to $125,000 by 2026.
The resolution aimed to redirect saved funds to services amid budget cuts, but faced opposition from most councilors.

title: HBO documentary series is scouting Cambridge for ‘unique’ neighbor stories
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: HBO's 'Neighbors' series is casting for its second season, focusing on disputes and relationships in Cambridge.
Producers are seeking unique stories involving noise, property lines, pets, and parking issues from Cambridge residents.
Season 1 featured disputes nationwide; residents can share their stories on the show's casting website.

title: The CapeFLYER train between Boston and Cape Cod returns for the summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: CapeFLYER train service resumes May 22, offering weekend trips between Boston's South Station and Hyannis until Labor Day.
CapeFLYER had over 10,000 ticket sales in 2025 with a 9% ridership increase, reflecting growing popularity.
Round-trip fare is $40, includes amenities like free wifi, bike racks, café car, and is pet-friendly and wheelchair-accessible.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about the forecast for Memorial Day weekend
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston's Memorial Day weekend forecast begins with dry weather, but rain is expected by Sunday.
Temperatures in Boston will drop to the 60s, with cooler breezes and cloudy skies expected.
Meteorologists predict rain on Sunday, drying out for Memorial Day; highest rain chance is Sunday.

title: Jarren Duran says he’s ‘willing to get hurt to make a play for my pitchers’ after Red Sox win
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jarren Duran hit a triple and a home run in the Red Sox's 4-3 win over the Royals.
Red Sox completed a three-game sweep against the Royals in Kansas City with a 4-3 victory.
Boston outfielder Jarren Duran made two crucial defensive plays, prioritizing team success over personal injury.
